title: Phaca serotina
intro: Phaca serotina, also known as the late purple lupin, is a perennial legume native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various ailments such as digestive issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Phaca serotina include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Phaca serotina is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often employed to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Phaca serotina is classified as a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of the southwestern United States and Mexico. The species name 'serotina' refers to its tendency to bloom in late summer. Phaca serotina is a perennial legume, typically growing to a height of 20-50 cm. It is a rare and understudied species, with limited information available in botanical literature.
